agents.hero_description: Build smart assistants for your business without writing code. The visual builder lets you ship an agent in hours instead of months.
agents.f1_desc: Assemble scenarios from ready-made blocks. No code — just connect elements on the canvas.
agents.f2_desc: Run agents where your customers are. Connect messengers in a few clicks.
agents.f3_desc: CRM, knowledge bases, external APIs — connect data sources without heavy engineering.
agents.f4_desc: Track agent performance, review chat history, and improve flows.
agents.b1_p: Ship your first agent today — no hiring developers or months of waiting.
agents.b2_p: One agent can replace 2–3 managers on routine tasks: answers, lead capture, consultations.
agents.b3_p: Agents do not sleep or take time off. Customers get answers any time.
agents.b4_p: Handle 10 or 10,000 conversations — cost does not explode with volume.
agents.b5_p: The agent does not get tired. Every customer gets the same level of service.
agents.b6_p: Edit flows in real time. Changes apply instantly.
agents.cta_subtitle: Create your first agent for free and see the results
agents.meta_title: AI Studio — AI agent builder | Humanitec
agents.meta_description: Build AI agents without code: visual builder, channels, integrations, and conversation analytics on Humanitec.
agents.faq1_q: Do we need engineers to launch an agent?
agents.faq1_a: No — flows are assembled from blocks on the canvas. APIs and Business-tier support cover advanced integrations.
agents.faq2_q: Where can agents talk to customers?
agents.faq2_a: Connect platform channels and on-site widgets so customers reach you where they already are.
agents.faq3_q: How do agents use company documents?
agents.faq3_a: Through Knowledge Base: upload policies and FAQs so answers cite your sources.

rag.hero_visual_alt: Knowledge Base screenshot: documents and search
rag.hero_description: Upload company documents so your AI agents can answer customers and employees using up-to-date information.
rag.f1_desc: PDF, Word, Excel, plain text — upload files in the formats you already use.
rag.f2_desc: Finds meaning, not just keywords. Ask questions in natural language.
rag.f3_desc: AI agents automatically use the knowledge base for accurate, relevant replies.
rag.f4_desc: Your documents stay isolated. Access is limited to your organization.
rag.benefits_title: How Knowledge Base helps your business
rag.b1_p: Staff and customers get information in seconds instead of digging through files for hours.
rag.b2_p: New hires ramp faster — the knowledge base answers recurring questions.
rag.b3_p: Routine questions are handled by an AI agent with the knowledge base. Humans take the hard cases.
rag.b4_p: Everything in one place. No conflicting document versions.
rag.b5_p: Upload a new document — agents immediately use the latest information.
rag.b6_p: Key employees' know-how stays in the company even when they leave.
rag.cta_title: Turn documents into a smart assistant
rag.cta_subtitle: Upload your first files and try search
rag.meta_title: Knowledge Base — semantic document search | Humanitec
rag.meta_description: Upload PDF and Word, search by meaning, and ground AI Studio agents on Humanitec.
rag.faq1_a: PDF, Word, Excel, text, and Markdown are indexed for semantic search.
rag.faq2_q: How does Knowledge Base connect to agents?
rag.faq2_a: AI Studio agents can answer using your uploaded policies and manuals.
rag.faq3_a: Inside your company perimeter on the platform, isolated between organizations.

crm.hero_description: Capture notes after calls and meetings — the system finds people, companies, and deals to build a graph of your business relationships.
crm.f1_desc: Write freely — AI extracts contacts, dates, tasks, and key topics.
crm.f2_desc: See how people, companies, and projects connect on an interactive map.
crm.f3_desc: Search by meaning: "what did we discuss with Ivan last month" — get a precise answer.
crm.f4_desc: Attach files to notes. AI indexes content for search.
crm.s1_p: After a call or meeting, write freely — what you discussed and agreed.
crm.s2_p: The system finds names, companies, dates, amounts, and creates links automatically.
crm.s3_p: Each note adds nodes and edges. You see the full relationship picture.
crm.s4_p: Ask in plain language and get a precise answer.
crm.b1_p: All communication history in one place. Who, when, what — in seconds.
crm.b2_p: Understand who knows whom, which companies are linked, who can introduce the right person.
crm.b3_p: No manual structuring. AI does it for you.
crm.b4_p: More notes means a smarter system. Knowledge compounds over years.
crm.b5_p: Share contacts and history with the team. New managers onboard faster.
crm.b6_p: Notes and contacts stay isolated. You control access.
crm.meta_title: NetWorkle — relationship graph and smart notebook | Humanitec
crm.meta_description: Notes, contacts, and deals link automatically; semantic search across company knowledge.
crm.faq1_a: NetWorkle is more flexible: free-form notes with automatic entities and graph relationships.
crm.faq2_a: Ask in natural language — semantic search runs across your notes.
crm.faq3_a: Yes — visibility is scoped to your company on the platform.

sync.hero_description: Channels, DMs, and threads in real time. Video with screen sharing, Git context next to the discussion, and notifications when you are away.
sync.f1_desc: Team channels, direct messages, and discussion threads — history is kept; updates arrive without reload.
sync.f2_desc: Calls from chat: camera, mic, screen share. Guest links for external participants.
sync.f3_desc: Tie discussions to repos and branches — the team sees what the code is about.
sync.f4_desc: If you are not in the open chat, the platform can remind you about new messages or mentions.
sync.s1_p: After sign-in you see your org's spaces and channels — data is isolated between companies.
sync.s2_p: Open a shared channel or DM a colleague; use threads for topics inside a channel.
sync.s3_p: Start a video call from chat: room participants join, you can share your screen.
sync.s4_p: History and attachments stay in the channel; notifications help when you are offline.
sync.b1_p: No switching between messenger and task tracker to discuss code and work.
sync.b2_p: Chats and files stay inside your organization on Humanitec.
sync.b3_p: See who is online to pick a good moment for a call.
sync.b4_p: Sync complements AI Studio, Knowledge Base, and NetWorkle — one entry from the dashboard.
sync.b5_p: Mention a teammate — they get a focused notification with context.
sync.b6_p: Share files in messages so agreements and artifacts stay in history.
sync.cta_subtitle: Sign in with your company account — chat opens from the dashboard.
sync.meta_title: Sync — team chat and video calls | Humanitec
sync.meta_description: Channels, threads, calls, and Git context alongside AI Studio and Knowledge Base.
sync.faq1_q: How is Sync different from a messenger?
sync.faq1_a: It is part of Humanitec: same users, company policies, and integrations.
sync.faq2_a: Yes — calls from chat with screen sharing and guest links.
sync.faq3_q: How do engineering discussions relate to code?
sync.faq3_a: Keep Git context next to channel discussions about tasks and PRs.

documents.gallery_2_alt: Humanitec Documents: app UI and collaboration
documents.hero_description: Collaborative Office editing: upload to S3, open on your document server, multiple authors in one file. Bindings per company and namespace — JWT for the editor and server secrets never reach the browser.
documents.f1_desc: The file list your team co-edits: company and active namespace via the BFF API.
documents.f2_desc: Launch from editor-config: several people edit the same document at once, using the collaboration mode your document server provides.
documents.f3_desc: Same isolation model as other products: X-Platform-Namespace scopes the document list.
documents.f4_desc: Download for the editor and callbacks use server-issued JWT; the client never sees MinIO or DS admin secrets.
documents.s1_p: Use your Humanitec session; the Documents app uses the same auth and company context — teammates open the same files for collaborative editing.
documents.s2_p: Set document_server_public_url and jwt_secret for your document server; the callback URL must be reachable from the DS container.
documents.s3_p: POST a file or an empty docx; the platform stores bytes in S3 and creates a binding row.
documents.s4_p: Open editor-config in the UI; teammates can work in the same file in parallel; the document server loads it via a time-limited download token.
documents.benefits_title: Why teams choose collaborative editing in Documents
documents.b1_p: Collaborative Word, Excel, and PowerPoint-style editing without building a custom editor.
documents.b2_p: No separate end-user account on Document Server for the main flow.
documents.b3_p: Available from the dashboard and apps menu next to chat, agents, and NetWorkle — documents and collaborative editing in one perimeter.
documents.b4_p: Namespaces keep unrelated teams from seeing each other's document lists.
documents.b5_p: Run Document Server beside the stack or on a dedicated host; adjust public URLs for browser and callbacks.
documents.b6_p: Integration status and misconfiguration surface via the BFF instead of failing silently.
documents.cta_subtitle: Sign in and open the app from the dashboard or this page — start collaborative document editing with your team.
documents.meta_description: Word, Excel, and PowerPoint on your document server with secure Humanitec BFF issuance.
documents.faq1_a: Document bytes live in your S3-compatible storage; the editor loads via short-lived server JWT.
documents.faq2_q: Do users need a separate editor login?
documents.faq2_a: The default flow signs in through Humanitec without a separate Document Server account.
documents.faq3_q: How do we isolate file lists?
documents.faq3_a: Use platform namespaces to separate teams and projects.
